tex:
(a) 
t
(i)  Alain and Beatrice share \$750 in the ratio Alain : Beatrice $= 8 : 7$.
\newline

Show that Alain receives \$400.
\newline

(b) Alain spends \$150.
\newline

Write \$150 as a percentage of \$400.
\newline

(c) He invests the remaining \$250 at a rate of 2\% per year simple interest.
\newline

Calculate the amount Alain has at the end of 5 years.
\newline

 Beatrice invests her \$350 at a rate of 0.25\% per **month** compound interest.
\newline

Calculate the amount Beatrice has at the end of 5 years.
\newline

Give your answer correct to the nearest dollar.
\newline

Carl, Dina and Eva share 100 oranges.
\newline

The ratio Carl's oranges : Dina's oranges $= 3 : 5$.
\newline

The ratio Carl's oranges : Eva's oranges $= 2 : 3$.
\newline

Find the number of oranges Carl receives.
\newline

Fred buys a house.
\newline

At the end of the first year, the value of the house increases by 5\%.
\newline

At the end of the second year, the value of the house increases by 3\% of its value at the end of the first year.
\newline

The value of Fred's house at the end of the second year is \$60564.
\newline

Calculate how much Fred paid for the house.
\newline

Gabrielle invests \$500 at a rate of $r$\% per year compound interest.
\newline

At the end of 8 years the value of Gabrielle's investment is \$609.20.
\newline

Find the value of $r$.
\newline

content.content:
Alain spends \$150.
\newline

Write \$150 as a percentage of \$400.
\newline

He invests the remaining \$250 at a rate of 2\% per year simple interest.
\newline

Calculate the amount Alain has at the end of 5 years.
\newline

_postParts.content:
\subpart Beatrice invests her \$350 at a rate of 0.25\% per \textbf{month} compound interest.

Calculate the amount Beatrice has at the end of 5 years.

Give your answer correct to the nearest dollar.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{3}

\end{subparts}

\part Carl, Dina and Eva share 100 oranges.

The ratio Carl's oranges : Dina's oranges $= 3 : 5$.

The ratio Carl's oranges : Eva's oranges $= 2 : 3$.

Find the number of oranges Carl receives.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}

\part Fred buys a house.

At the end of the first year, the value of the house increases by 5\%.

At the end of the second year, the value of the house increases by 3\% of its value at the end of the first year.

The value of Fred's house at the end of the second year is \$60564.

Calculate how much Fred paid for the house.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{3}

\part Gabrielle invests \$500 at a rate of $r$\% per year compound interest.

At the end of 8 years the value of Gabrielle's investment is \$609.20.

Find the value of $r$.

\AnswerLine[$r =$][]
\Marks{3}

\end{parts}

tex:
(a) 
100 students take part in a reaction test.
\newline

The table shows the results.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Reaction time (seconds) & 6 & 7 & 8 & 9 & 10 & 11 \\
\hline
Number of students & 3 & 32 & 19 & 29 & 11 & 6 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(i)  Write down the mode.
\newline

 Find the median.
\newline

 Calculate the mean.
\newline

 Two students are chosen at random.
\newline

Find the probability that both their reaction times are greater than or equal to 9 seconds.
\newline

The box-and-whisker plot shows the heights, $h\,\text{cm}$, of some students.
\newline

(i)  Find the range.
\newline

 Find the interquartile range.
\newline

The mass of each of 200 potatoes is measured.
\newline

The table shows the results.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Mass ($m$ grams) & $50 < m \leqslant 110$ & $110 < m \leqslant 200$ & $200 < m \leqslant 300$ \\
\hline
Frequency & 60 & 99 & 41 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(i)  Calculate an estimate of the mean.
\newline

 Complete the histogram to show the information in the table.
\newline

tex:
The diagram shows a cylinder containing water.
\newline

There is a solid metal sphere touching the base of the cylinder.
\newline

Half of the sphere is in the water.
\newline

The radius of the cylinder is $12\,\text{cm}$ and the radius of the sphere is $3\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

(a) 
The sphere is removed from the cylinder and the level of the water decreases by $h\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

Show that $h = 0.125$.
\newline

[The volume, $V$, of a sphere with radius $r$ is $V = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$.
\newline
]

The water in the cylinder is poured into another cylinder of radius $R\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

The depth of the water in this cylinder is $18\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

Calculate the value of $R$.
\newline

The sphere is melted down and some of the metal is used to make 30 cubes with edge length $1.5\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

Calculate the percentage of metal **not** used.
\newline

[The volume, $V$, of a sphere with radius $r$ is $V = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$.
\newline
]

\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

_rawBody:
\relinput{Figures/Q03.tex}

The diagram shows a cylinder containing water.

There is a solid metal sphere touching the base of the cylinder.

Half of the sphere is in the water.

The radius of the cylinder is 12\,cm and the radius of the sphere is 3\,cm.

\begin{parts}
\part The sphere is removed from the cylinder and the level of the water decreases by $h$\,cm.

Show that $h = 0.125$.

[The volume, $V$, of a sphere with radius $r$ is $V = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$.]
\Marks{3}

\part The water in the cylinder is poured into another cylinder of radius $R$\,cm.

The depth of the water in this cylinder is 18\,cm.

Calculate the value of $R$.

\AnswerLine[$R =$][]
\Marks{3}

\part The sphere is melted down and some of the metal is used to make 30 cubes with edge length 1.5\,cm.

Calculate the percentage of metal \textbf{not} used.

[The volume, $V$, of a sphere with radius $r$ is $V = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$.]

\AnswerLine[][\%]
\Marks{3}

\end{parts}

tex:
(a) 
t

(i)  Enlarge triangle $T$ by scale factor 3, centre $(0, 2)$.
\newline

(b) Rotate triangle $T$ about $(4, 2)$ by $90°$ clockwise.
\newline

Label the image $P$.
\newline

(c) Reflect triangle $T$ in the line $x + y = 6$.
\newline

Label the image $Q$.
\newline

(d) Describe fully the **single** transformation that maps triangle $P$ onto triangle $Q$.
\newline

t

The diagram shows triangle $OHK$, where $O$ is the origin.
\newline

The position vector of $H$ is $\mathbf{a}$ and the position vector of $K$ is $\mathbf{b}$.
\newline

$Z$ is the point on $HK$ such that $HZ : ZK = 2 : 5$.
\newline

Find the position vector of $Z$, in terms of $\mathbf{a}$ and $\mathbf{b}$.
\newline

Give your answer in its simplest form.
\newline

\newline

\newline

_postParts.content:
The diagram shows triangle $OHK$, where $O$ is the origin.

The position vector of $H$ is $\mathbf{a}$ and the position vector of $K$ is $\mathbf{b}$.

$Z$ is the point on $HK$ such that $HZ : ZK = 2 : 5$.

Find the position vector of $Z$, in terms of $\mathbf{a}$ and $\mathbf{b}$.

Give your answer in its simplest form.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{3}

\end{parts}

tex:

 Rearrange the formula to write $v$ in terms of $s$, $u$ and $t$.

\newline

(i)  $2qt - 3t - 6 + 4q$

 $x^3 - 25x$

tex:
$\mathcal{E} = \{\text{students in a class}\}$   $P = \{\text{students who study Physics}\}$   $C = \{\text{students who study Chemistry}\}$

$\text{n}(\mathcal{E}) = 24$   $\text{n}(P) = 17$   $\text{n}(C) = 14$   $\text{n}(P \cap C) = 9$

(a) 
Complete the Venn diagram.
\newline

t
(i)  Find $\text{n}(P \cap C')$.
\newline

 Find $\text{n}(P \cup C')$.
\newline

Two students are picked from the class at random.
\newline

Find the probability that one student studies both subjects and one student studies Chemistry but not Physics.
\newline

Two of the students who study Physics are picked at random.
\newline

Find the probability that they both study Chemistry.
\newline

content.content:

stem.content:
$\mathcal{E} = \{\text{students in a class}\}$ \quad $P = \{\text{students who study Physics}\}$ \quad $C = \{\text{students who study Chemistry}\}$

$\text{n}(\mathcal{E}) = 24$ \quad $\text{n}(P) = 17$ \quad $\text{n}(C) = 14$ \quad $\text{n}(P \cap C) = 9$

qtype: function evaluation, solving equations with functions, composite functions, inverse functions
tex:
\[f(x) = 2x - 1 \qquad g(x) = 3x + 2 \qquad h(x) = \frac{1}{x}, \, x \neq 0 \qquad j(x) = x^2\]

(a) 
Find $\text{j}(-1)$.
\newline

Find $x$ when $\text{f}(x) + \text{g}(x) = 0$.
\newline

Find $\text{gg}(x)$, giving your answer in its simplest form.
\newline

Find $\text{hf}(x) + \text{gh}(x)$, giving your answer as a single fraction in its simplest form.
\newline

When $\text{pp}(x) = x$, $\text{p}(x)$ is a function such that $\text{p}^{-1}(x) = \text{p}(x)$.
\newline

Draw a ring around the function that has this property.
\newline

\[f(x) = 2x - 1 \qquad g(x) = 3x + 2 \qquad h(x) = \frac{1}{x}, \, x \neq 0 \qquad j(x) = x^2\]
